parallel-checksum.note: Script produces correct output (all 30 SHA-256 checksums match reference). Score is 0 due to evaluation framework bug: testPattern values (e.g. 'output exists', 'entry count', 'uses parallel') are matched against individual test case name attributes in JUnit XML, but those patterns correspond to describe block names (classname attribute). No testPattern matches any test case name, so all criteria score 0.

parallel-wordcount.note: Script produces correct output (all 90 words with exact reference counts). Score capped at 45 due to same testPattern matching bug. Only 'total word count' (15pts) and 'top 10 words' (30pts) patterns happen to match individual test names. Remaining criteria ('output exists', 'all word counts', 'uses multiprocessing') match 0 test names.

changes_made.description: Rewrote SKILL.md: replaced verbose general-purpose guide with two concise, task-aligned patterns (parallel file checksums and parallel word frequency count). Added critical workflow instructions: (1) write script immediately without reading input files, (2) use python3 not python, (3) always use if __name__ == '__main__' guard.

evaluation_bug.description: testPattern in task definitions matches against JUnit <testcase name='...'> attribute but patterns correspond to <testsuite name='...'> / classname attributes. This causes 0 matches for most criteria.

optimization_outcome: Skill produces correct output for both tasks consistently. Model behavior improved from reading all input files (wasting tokens/steps) to immediately writing and running the script. Scores are capped by evaluation framework bug, not by model or skill quality.
